data.body: PASS-1 review note (G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection): No G6 findings. This chunk is the docs-only ticket "Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern". G6 is effectively not-applicable: documentation authoring is a well-understood activity with no structurally-different "better approach" to coach (no data-layer / control-flow / dependency-graph alternative exists for "write docs about X"). The plan also affirmatively satisfies the criterion's correctness concern: its own AC#4 ("States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)") directly forecloses the misleading-latency anti-pattern that 5fd2's round-5 review flagged (presenting the ~50ms fast-claim number as user-facing time-to-first-work when the heavy review runs out-of-band, ~minutes for a heavy epic). The approach correctly anchors on the canonical workflow-engine doc in 6f2d (WS-DOC) by cross-reference rather than duplicating content, avoiding a divergence/duplication anti-pattern, and states a positive rationale ("the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented"). Clean for G6.
